channel.title: Contextual Mac Translator
channel.description: Auto-update feed for Contextual Mac Translator. Sparkle 2 + EdDSA signed.

item.title: v1.0.0
item.description:
First stable release

v1.0.0 is a commitment to API + UX stability, not a re-implementation. Bundle is byte-identical in behaviour to v0.11.1 — same code path, same backend wire format, same prompts, same hotkey UX. The 1.0.0 number marks the boundary where:

  • Feature set is complete for the workflows the app was built for: inbound translate, outbound translate (per-persona), contextual rewrite (per-tone), tone picker, OCR capture, App Intents (Shortcuts.app + Spotlight + Siri), Prompt Engineer mode.
  • Dual-mode translation backend: bring-your-own-API direct providers (Gemini, OpenAI-compatible, Ollama, DeepL, Google Translate, LibreTranslate) and SaaS-hosted Contextual MT Cloud with quota / device cap / per-user encrypted cache.
  • Distribution pipeline is hardened end-to-end: Apple Developer-ID signed, Apple-notarized, stapled, AppleDouble-clean ZIP + DMG, Sparkle EdDSA auto-update, GitHub Pages workflow deploy for the appcast.
  • 400 tests guard the public contract.

From v1.0.0 onwards: feature additions land as minor bumps (1.1.0 …), bug fixes as patch bumps (1.0.1 …). On-disk layout (UserDefaults + Keychain) and backend wire format are now considered stable surface area; any breaking change earns a major bump (2.0.0).

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.11.1
item.description:
Rewrite pronoun-preservation hotfix

User-reported bug: a draft addressed with "em" was coming back as "anh/chị" (or vice versa) after a rewrite, inverting the entire social relationship. In Vietnamese this is a major correctness failure — pronouns ARE the relationship.

  • Mac app PromptBuilder.rewriteSystemPrompt: pronoun-preservation rule promoted to an ABSOLUTE top-level section with per-pronoun guarantees ("em" stays "em", "tôi" stays "tôi", every addressing pronoun preserved verbatim). 2 broken few-shot examples fixed; 1 new example demonstrates "em → anh" fidelity.
  • Supabase REWRITE_SYSTEM_PROMPT: previously had no pronoun rule at all. Now mirrors PromptBuilder so SaaS users get the same quality (already deployed).
  • Other-language formality markers ("tu"/"vous", "tú"/"usted", "你"/"您", "ты"/"вы") added so the fix isn't VN-only.

400 tests pass. UserDefaults + Keychain layout unchanged from v0.11.0.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.11.0
item.description:
Prompt Engineer mode

Type a minimal Vietnamese keyword sketch of a coding task, press a hotkey, and the app pastes back a complete, well-structured English prompt for Claude Code, Codex, ChatGPT, or Claude Desktop. Same hotkey machinery as translate/rewrite, with a dedicated server prompt + temperature tuned for expansion.

  • Settings → Prompt Engineer — new section with per-binding rows: name, target language, optional custom expansion guidelines, hotkey recorder.
  • TranslationDirection.expand + PromptBinding — new data types; persist under translator.promptBindings.
  • Dedicated system prompt + temperature 0.6 — Supabase Edge Function (already deployed) routes direction: "expand" to EXPAND_SYSTEM_PROMPT; direct-API providers get the matching prompt via PromptBuilder.

400 tests pass (+10 new). UserDefaults adds one new key (promptBindings) defaulting to empty; users opt in by adding a binding.

See release notes on GitHub or the recipe at contextmt.dev/recipes/prompt-expander.

item.title: v0.10.6
item.description:
Rewrite works on SaaS backend

User-reported bug: pressing a tone rewrite hotkey (Polite / Professional / Friendly etc.) did nothing when the app was configured to use Contextual MT Cloud or a self-hosted backend. The hotkey registration was silently skipped because the historical rewriteAvailable gate hardcoded "only direct-API providers can rewrite".

  • Mac app: SettingsStore.rewriteAvailable now returns true for both customBackend and firstPartyBackend translation sources.
  • SaaS backend: the Supabase translate Edge Function accepts a new direction: "rewrite" field that switches Gemini to a same-language tone-rewrite system prompt (already deployed). Cache key includes direction so a translate-of-X never returns a previously-cached rewrite-of-X.

Backward-compatible: older Mac builds and older self-hosted backends keep working in translate-only mode. UserDefaults + Keychain layout unchanged from v0.10.5.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.10.5
item.description:
Window chrome hotfix

Three app windows (Onboarding, Settings, What's New) were carrying a transparent-titlebar / clear-background combination from an earlier macOS 26 "Liquid Glass" experiment. On real macOS the window title text rendered straight onto the desktop wallpaper with no contrast — unreadable.

  • Standard opaque titlebar restored on Onboarding, Settings, and What's New windows. The SwiftUI content's Liquid Glass material still applies to the content area on macOS 26; only the titlebar chrome changes.

UserDefaults + Keychain layout unchanged from v0.10.4. 390 tests pass.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.10.4
item.description:
Permission UX cleanup

Drops Input Monitoring from the onboarding and Settings surface entirely — the app never actually needed it, and the Request button could not recover after the first denial (macOS silently suppresses the prompt forever once dismissed).

  • Input Monitoring removed — Carbon RegisterEventHotKey (global hotkeys) and CGEvent posting (paste) both run on Accessibility alone. Asking for a permission we never call into was pure friction.
  • Auto-open Settings fallback — when the Accessibility request prompt is silently suppressed by macOS (after a prior denial), the app now opens System Settings → Accessibility automatically after a 1.5-second grace period. No more "I clicked Request and nothing happened" dead end.

UserDefaults + Keychain layout unchanged from v0.10.3. 390 tests pass.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.10.3
item.description:
Distribution hotfix — Gatekeeper acceptance

v0.10.0, v0.10.1, and v0.10.2 all shipped with hidden AppleDouble metadata files (._*) inside the embedded Sparkle.framework — invisible to codesign --sign, so the build looked fine, but on the user's machine codesign --verify --deep --strict rejected the bundle as "a sealed resource is missing or invalid". Gatekeeper refused to launch the app, and Sparkle's Installer.xpc failed verification mid-update — surfacing as "app chưa sign" when users tried to upgrade.

v0.10.3 is the v0.10.2 codebase re-packaged with the build pipeline fixed.

  • Strip ._* before AND after codesign — find … -name "._*" -delete + dot_clean -m at both points in package_app.sh.
  • Distribution ZIP uses ditto --norsrc --noextattr --noacl — even though the source bundle is clean, ditto would otherwise re-add ._* files into the ZIP from extended attributes on disk. The new flags skip xattr packaging entirely.
  • Verified end-to-end — extracting the v0.10.3 ZIP yields a bundle where codesign --verify --deep --strict passes and spctl --assess reports accepted source=Notarized Developer ID.

If Sparkle's Check for Updates fails to install v0.10.3 (likely if v0.10.0 → v0.10.2 was aborted mid-flight), download the v0.10.3 ZIP manually from the release page and drag the .app into /Applications replacing the old copy. Future Sparkle updates from v0.10.3 onwards work normally.

item.title: v0.10.2
item.description:
Permission UX & TCC stability

A user-reported pain point: every Sparkle upgrade felt like macOS was clearing the app's Accessibility grant — translate hotkeys went silent until you manually re-granted, with no in-app surface telling you what happened. v0.10.2 fixes the experience and hardens the signature so the underlying TCC issue is much less likely to recur.

  • Auto-recovery onboarding — on launch, the app detects when Accessibility was previously granted but is no longer active (a TCC reset signal). When detected, the onboarding panel pops automatically with copy that acknowledges you already did this once.
  • Codesign hardening — --identifier is now passed explicitly to codesign so the designated requirement TCC keys on is byte-stable across releases. Verified: v0.10.0, v0.10.1, and v0.10.2 all sign with byte-identical designated requirements.
  • Faster grant detection — the post-request settle delay bumps 1s → 2s so the system prompt's "Allow" click registers reliably without needing the onboarding window's polling loop to catch up.

11 new tests pin the recovery decision tree + PermissionManager grant-sync behaviour. 388 tests pass total.

UserDefaults layout stays compatible — one new key (translator.lastKnownAccessibilityGranted) defaults to false on existing installs so the recovery flow does not fire spuriously on the v0.10.1 → v0.10.2 upgrade itself.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.10.1
item.description:
SaaS backend hotfix

v0.10.0 shipped just as the production SaaS Edge Function went live, and the two wire formats turned out to be incompatible. Translates against app.contextmt.dev failed immediately with HTTP 400, then HTTP 200 but "missing translation" once that cleared. v0.10.1 makes both work.

  • Dual wire format — every request body now carries each routing field under both camelCase (legacy self-hosted FastAPI) and snake_case (SaaS Supabase Edge Function). One payload, both backends.
  • Streaming graceful fallback — the streaming flow now sniffs Content-Type; if the backend returns one-shot JSON instead of text/event-stream (Supabase does this), the body is decoded as a single translation and emitted as one done update. Self-hosted SSE keeps real streaming.

UserDefaults + Keychain layout byte-identical to v0.10.0 — risk-free upgrade.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.10.0
item.description:
Cultural Precision & Privacy

Three new sub-features anchored on "Apple Intelligence can't do per-locale precision":

  • VN Register Card — pin dialect (Bắc/Nam), kinship (anh/chị/em/cháu/bạn), formality. Every rewrite + outbound translate matches.
  • Privacy badge — PreviewHUD shows 🛡 Local / ☁ Cloud / 🏢 Hosted per translation. Settings → Privacy has an Ollama install helper + test-connection.
  • Glossary v2 — three typed entry kinds (Don't translate / Alias / Always translate) replace the free-text blob. Legacy rules still flow underneath.

Storage byte-identical to v0.9.x — every setting + binding + hotkey round-trips clean.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.9.2
item.description:
Second refactor patch

No new features. Clears the remaining W1 (HIGH), MED-4, and LOW-2 items from the v0.9.0 deliver-phase review.

  • Internal: Supabase / SaaS surface extracted from SettingsStore into a new SaaSConfig file. SettingsStore drops from 593 → 538 LOC; the Supabase concept count it owns goes from 6 to 1.
  • Internal: ScreenCaptureService protocol shape aligned to OCREngine (plain Sendable, no @MainActor on the protocol). Cleans up the asymmetry from v0.9.0 P3.
  • Polish: picker + capture hotkey recorder sheets now share one helper. Pre-allocated HotkeyConfig defaults replace the per-render allocations the v0.9.0 review flagged.

Risk-free upgrade — same 319 tests pass with the same test bodies; UserDefaults + Keychain layout byte-identical to v0.9.1.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.9.1
item.description:
Refactor + reliability patch

No new features. Two structural-debt fixes the v0.9.0 deliver-phase review flagged + 13 high-leverage tests for previously-silent fallback paths.

  • Internal: TranslationWorkflow split from 876 → 630 LOC. New CaptureOrchestrator + RewriteService modules. Behaviour byte-identical.
  • Fix: the menu-bar "Send Keigo / Send Casual" items no longer silently fall back to Japanese for a VN-primary user — now surfaces an actionable error pointing to Settings.
  • Tests: +13 (BackendProvider.streamingURL path rewriter + WhatsNew per-version highlight gating).

Upgrade is risk-free: all v0.9.0 user state, hotkeys, bindings, and the multi-variant / expressive-tones toggles round-trip clean.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.9.0
item.description:
Input Surface Expansion — Shortcuts.app + OCR-from-screen

Two new ways to call the translator. Both are opt-in.

  • Shortcuts.app, Spotlight, Siri — three new App Intents (Translate Text, Rewrite with Tone, Rewrite with Instruction) let you chain the translator into any workflow. Compose them with Raycast, Alfred, Quick Actions, Calendar automations, anything that speaks AppIntents.
  • OCR-from-screen translate — bind a hotkey in Settings → Capture, drag the system crosshair over any text on screen, and the app reads it (on-device Vision: vi / en / zh-Hans / ja / ko) and translates it for you. Designed for Aliwangwang / 1688 / Messenger chats where browser translation breaks.

What's-New window pops once on first launch to point you at both. The app stays in the background — no focus stolen.

Zero-migration: every v0.8.5 setting, hotkey, binding, glossary, expressive-tones / multi-variant toggle round-trips clean.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.8.5
item.description:
Multi-variant rewrite — 3 drafts in one round-trip

Opt-in setting that asks the LLM for three different rewrites in a single call. The preview HUD pages through them with ← / → arrows, ⌘1–3 quick-select, and saves each draft's edits separately as you flip back and forth.

Enable in Settings → Contextual rewrite → "Generate 3 drafts per rewrite". Costs ~1.5–2× tokens per rewrite but only one network round-trip.

Falls back gracefully: if the model ignores the multi-variant prompt or its drafts are filtered as refusals, you get a single rewrite via the existing retry chain. Works with all LLM providers (Gemini, Ollama, OpenAI-compatible).

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.8.4
item.description:
Pre-warmed picker + per-binding "In picker" + VoiceOver polish

Three small upgrades that make the tone picker feel native:

  • Pre-warmed panel — the picker NSPanel is built at app launch instead of on first hotkey press. First open is now near-instant.
  • "In picker" per binding — every saved rewrite binding in Settings now has an opt-in checkbox to also surface it as a row in the popup. Pick saved instructions without remembering hotkeys.
  • VoiceOver — every picker row is properly labelled with its ⌘-shortcut hint and selected state; the container announces "Type to filter, arrow keys to navigate, Return to apply, Escape to cancel".

Zero-migration: bindings persisted by v0.8.3 decode with In Picker = ON by default.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.8.3
item.description:
Free-text custom instruction in the picker

Type anything into the picker filter and a "Use: …" row appears at the top. Picking it rewrites with your typed instruction instead of a preset tone — Apple Writing Tools "Describe your change" style, kept inside the same hotkey flow.

Examples: "make it sound less defensive", "warmer reply to an angry client", "shorter, under 2 sentences".

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.8.2
item.description:
New tone: "Chửi thề" (casual-raw)

Opt-in Vietnamese close-friends register. Uses profanity markers like vl / vcl / đm as natural intensifiers (the "as hell" / "af" of Vietnamese internet writing), preferring abbreviated forms. Identity-safe — never adds slurs or attacks identity.

Hidden behind Settings → Contextual rewrite → Enable expressive tones (Chửi thề) with a confirmation dialog. Routes through Gemini with safetySettings = BLOCK_NONE so the model doesn't refuse profanity-flavoured drafts. Other providers may refuse — the existing refusal-detection retry kicks in.

For chats with friends, not customers. Every rewrite still shows in preview before sending.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.8.1
item.description:
Tone picker polish (v0.8.0 → v0.8.1)

Code-review polish on top of v0.8.0. No behaviour change — pure cleanup:

  • Picker panel size lives in one place now (was duplicated between construction and positioning, with a drift risk).
  • Removed an unused selection-clamp helper that wasn't actually called from the view.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.8.0
item.description:
Tone picker hotkey — one hotkey, every tone

Bind ONE global hotkey to open a popup picker listing every rewrite tone: Polite, Professional, Friendly, Firm-but-polite, De-escalate, Concise, Custom. Pick with arrow keys + Return, type-to-filter, or ⌘ 1-7 quick-select. The app rewrites the current input line in that tone, shows the preview HUD, pastes when you confirm.

Hybrid with v0.7's per-binding model — both coexist. Power users keep their muscle-memory hotkeys; the picker scales to the long tail.

Privacy-aware: refuses to capture from password fields (AXSecureTextField), so a draft never leaks into LLM logs.

Setup: Settings → Contextual rewrite → "Tone picker hotkey" → record your shortcut. Needs an LLM provider (Gemini, Ollama, OpenAI-compatible).

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.7.1
item.description:
Polish for v0.7.0 Contextual Rewrite

Two post-ship review fixes:

  • Clipboard race on post-paste focus loss — the workflow now uses the 700 ms delayed restore so a slow target app can't read the snapshot before consuming the just-pasted text.
  • Rewrite hotkeys are now only registered when the active provider can rewrite (Gemini, Ollama, OpenAI-compatible). Switch to DeepL or Google Translate and the rewrite hotkey unregisters cleanly; switch back and it returns.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.7.0
item.description:
Contextual rewrite — same-language tone shift on a hotkey

Bind a hotkey to a tone (Polite, Professional, De-escalate, Friendly, Firm-but-polite, Concise, or a free-text custom tone) and the app rewrites the current input line in that tone, in the SAME language. Built for the moment you typed something blunt or angry and need to soften it before sending. Always shows a preview HUD before sending — never auto-paste.

Vietnamese-aware: the prompt preserves your anh/chị/em pronouns and adds politeness particles (ạ / dạ / nhé) as the tone requires.

Setup: Settings → "Contextual rewrite" → Add binding. Needs an LLM provider (Gemini, Ollama, OpenAI-compatible); DeepL and Google Translate cannot rewrite — Settings warns when the active provider can't.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.6.3
item.description:
Real Liquid Glass for the app windows

The onboarding and Settings windows now use the actual macOS 26 Liquid Glass API (.glassEffect()) instead of the legacy vibrancy material.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.6.2
item.description:
Liquid Glass Settings + OTA verification

The Settings window now adopts the macOS 26 Liquid Glass look, matching the onboarding window. This release also verifies the Sparkle auto-update path end-to-end.

See release notes on GitHub.

item.title: v0.6.1
item.description:
Crash hotfix + draggable popups

v0.6.0 crashed on launch (a missing rpath meant the embedded Sparkle framework could not load). This release fixes that.

  • Fixed: app no longer crashes at launch
  • HUD and preview popups are now draggable and resizable
  • Onboarding window adopts the macOS 26 Liquid Glass look

See release notes on GitHub.
